escalation.rationale: US launched fresh strikes on Iran following an Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, Iran retaliated with strikes on Jordan and Kuwait, and a fragile ceasefire framework is under severe stress alongside continued Russian advances in eastern Ukraine and a large German drone package announced for Kyiv.

ticker_items: US strikes Iran after vessel set ablaze in Strait of Hormuz; Iran hits Jordan, Kuwait.
Iran publishes revenge target list naming Trump and European leaders after Khamenei killing.
Germany funds 50,000 Shrike FPV strike drones for Ukraine in landmark purchase.
Russia advances on Kostyantynivka; ISW reports shift to attrition tactics.
Senator Lindsey Graham dies suddenly at 71; key US foreign policy hawk gone.

new_entrant.text: Jordan and Kuwait were struck by Iranian retaliatory attacks on July 12, 2026, marking their direct entry into the kinetic US-Iran conflict zone.

eastern_europe.description: Russian forces advanced on Kostyantynivka, a key city in Ukraine's eastern fortress belt, as part of ongoing offensive operations.

middle_east.description: The United States launched fresh airstrikes on Iran early July 12 in response to an Iranian strike on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran struck sites in Jordan as a retaliatory response to renewed US military action, threatening the fragile ceasefire framework.
Iran struck sites in Kuwait as part of its retaliatory strikes following US military action on July 12.
Qatar condemned Iranian assaults on its territory and other Arab nations amid the widening 2026 Iran war.
A senior Iranian military officer was killed in joint US-Israeli strikes amid renewed 2026 hostilities.

today.kia: ~3,468 in Iran; ~3,696 in Lebanon; ~28 in Gulf states

today.incident: Iranian officer killed in US-Israeli strike
Russian military KIA (1,600th day cumulative claim)
Hodeidah fighting (Houthis vs Yemeni forces, early July)

claimed_totals.claim: Deaths in Iran from US-Israel Operation Epic Fury
Deaths in Lebanon from US-Israel strikes
Deaths in Gulf states from US-Israel strikes
Russian soldiers KIA in Ukraine (1,600th day)

russia.text: Russia has no plans to attack NATO and is open to equal dialogue; NATO justifies its preparations with the false claim that Russia poses a threat.
russia.speaker: Vladislav Maslennikov
russia.title: Director, Russian MFA Department of European Issues

middle_east.text: Mojtaba Khamenei vowed retribution for the attacks after the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.
middle_east.speaker: Mojtaba Khamenei
middle_east.title: Son of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei

middle_east.text: Qatar condemns Iranian assaults on its land and other Arab nations amid the 2026 Iran war.
middle_east.speaker: Qatar Government
middle_east.title: Official statement

observers.description: Germany announced funding for 50,000 Shrike FPV attack drones for Ukraine, featuring autonomous target-tracking software. This represents one of the largest single drone procurement packages by any Western government to date.

The US launched fresh strikes on Iran on July 12 following an Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Washington separately announced provision of Patriot air defense system licenses to Ukraine. Senator Lindsey Graham, a key foreign policy hawk and Trump ally, died suddenly at 71, creating uncertainty around US posture.

Turkey hosted the NATO summit in Ankara and is deepening ties with the US, with reports of potential F-35 sales under discussion. Turkey has also shifted its diplomatic stance, targeting Netanyahu amid ongoing Israel tensions, raising concerns among NATO allies Greece and Israel.

Open-source reports indicate North Korea is supplying approximately 40% of Russia's artillery ammunition. China and North Korea marked the 65th anniversary of their friendship treaty, with Xi urging both nations to maintain 'strategic resolve' amid global turmoil.

Xi Jinping called on China and North Korea to keep strategic resolve amid global turbulence. China continues to avoid direct military involvement while managing the China-North Korea alliance and monitoring the Iran crisis.

Iran struck a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, triggering fresh US retaliation. Tehran then struck sites in Jordan and Kuwait in further escalation. A conservative Iranian newspaper published a revenge target list naming Trump and European leaders following the killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei.

Qatar condemned Iranian attacks on its territory and on other Arab nations. Qatar's founding emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani died, creating domestic focus during an extremely volatile regional period.

financial.notes: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains well below pre-war levels and global oil inventories continue to be drawn down amid the ongoing US-Iran military exchanges, sustaining upward pressure on energy prices.

update_log.text: Update run initiated for 2026-07-13; escalation score set at 8.7 reflecting ongoing US-Iran exchanges and Ukrainian front developments.
US launches fresh airstrikes on Iran following Iranian attack on container ship in Strait of Hormuz.
Iran retaliates with strikes on sites in Jordan and Kuwait, further threatening fragile ceasefire.
Iranian officer killed in joint US-Israeli strike amid renewed 2026 hostilities.
Qatar condemns Iranian assaults on its territory and other Arab states.
Conservative Iranian newspaper publishes revenge target list naming Trump and multiple European leaders.
Germany funds 50,000 Shrike FPV attack drones for Ukraine in landmark procurement.
Russia advances on Kostyantynivka; ISW reports shift to attrition tactics in eastern Ukraine.
Senator Lindsey Graham dies suddenly at 71; Trump and world leaders pay tribute.
Al Jazeera casualty tracker records ~3,468 dead in Iran, ~3,696 in Lebanon, ~28 in Gulf states from US-Israel strikes (last updated 2026-06-10).
War on the Rocks analyst piece examines sorry record of US regime change operations in Middle East, referencing Operation Epic Fury launched Feb 28 2026.
